data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Netflix's system is built on a microservices architecture to improve scalability and manageability.
data.technical_spec: Decomposed services communicating over RESTful APIs, built with various languages like Java, Node.js, Python.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Netflix employs a global network of CDNs to ensure efficient media content delivery.
data.technical_spec: Leveraging Open Connect appliance located in ISP data centers for optimized streaming.

data.title: Cloud Hosting on AWS
data.description: Netflix is hosted on Amazon Web Services, utilizing its vast, scalable infrastructure.
data.technical_spec: AWS services including EC2, S3, and Lambda for cloud operations and database management.

data.title: Data Management System
data.description: Netflix uses complex data management systems for analytics and personalization.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes Cassandra for high availability and resilience, alongside Elasticsearch for real-time applications.

data.title: Server-Side APIs
data.description: APIs are used extensively for handling user requests and delivering personalized recommendations.
data.technical_spec: Server-side handlers in languages like Java and Node.js communicating with backend services.
